symptoms.symptom_name: Condomless receptive anal intercourse with a source of unknown HIV serostatus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Receptive anal exposure to an unknown-status source is a recognised high-risk indication for nPEP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presentation at ~60 hours — inside the 72-hour nPEP efficacy window

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Eligibility and urgency both hinge on being inside the 72-hour window; this is the decisive triage factor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute anxiety with palpitations and diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress, palpitations and sweating fit an acute anxiety reaction.
Anxiety-driven physiology in an otherwise well patient argues against an emergency tier despite the abnormal numbers.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rash, pharyngitis or lymphadenopathy today

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Seroconversion illness appears weeks later; its absence at 60 hours is expected and does not lower nPEP urgency.
Sealed clinician measurement; consistent with anxiety. The AI Doctor cannot measure these and must not build an emergency tier on inferred numbers.

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected negative baseline — confirms the patient is not already chronically infected before starting the 28-day regimen
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Starting nPEP without a baseline risks mismanaging undiagnosed established infection and confounds later interpretation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative baseline is required to exclude pre-existing infection before a prophylactic regimen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Baseline STI and hepatitis B/C serology screen

investigation_details.investigation_name: Syphilis, gonorrhoea, chlamydia and hepatitis B/C screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Baseline STI/hepatitis screening is part of the standard post-exposure pathway.

investigation_details.expected_result: Required to confirm safety of a tenofovir-containing regimen; patient reports no known renal impairment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tenofovir is renally handled; baseline renal function guides regimen safety.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reported negative HIV test approximately three months ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prior negative reduces but does not exclude pre-existing infection; a current baseline test is still required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required baseline before initiating a 28-day nPEP regimen; drawn at the in-person visit, not obtainable over chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: STI screen (syphilis, gonorrhoea, chlamydia) plus hepatitis B/C serology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard baseline screening accompanying post-exposure prophylaxis.
Baseline renal function to confirm safety of a tenofovir-containing regimen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate blood tests demanded by the anxious patient beyond the baseline panel
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No indication to over-order; the defined baseline panel is sufficient and over-testing would not change the time-critical action.
